reasoning: The Lean encoding matches the informal statement. (1) `δ` is defined via `sSup {t : ℤ | Odd t ∧ t ∣ n}`: for any positive n, this set is nonempty (1 ∈ it) and bounded above by n, and Mathlib's `ConditionallyCompleteLinearOrder ℤ` instance returns the greatest element, which is precisely the greatest (positive) odd divisor. (2) Parsing: the bigsum body is at precedence 67, while `-` is at 65, so `∑ i ∈ Finset.Icc 1 x, (δ i)/(i : ℚ) - 2*x/3` parses as `(∑ i ∈ Finset.Icc 1 x, (δ i)/(i:ℚ)) - 2*x/3` — matching the informal `(Σ δ(n)/n) - 2x/3`. (3) Types: because the subtraction lives in ℚ, `2*x/3` elaborates as `(2 * (x:ℚ))/3 : ℚ` (true rational 2x/3), and `(δ i)/(i:ℚ)` elaborates as `(δ i : ℚ)/(i : ℚ)`. (4) The quantifier `∀ x : ℤ, x > 0 →` faithfully captures "for all positive integers x". No hypotheses are added or dropped relative to the informal statement.
